title: Header nav wraps to a second line and crowds the wordmark at 390px
evidence: VERIFIED in pm-home__m-fold.png, rm-home__m-fold.png and pm-dropoff-heathrow__m-fold.png. The first nav label ('Drop-off charges' on ParkMath, 'Roaming charges' on RoamMath) wraps — 'Drop-off' sits on one line and 'charges' drops to a second line directly beneath, pushing the nav block hard against the 'ParkMath'/'RoamMath' wordmark on the left. The nav genuinely renders on two lines beside a single-line logo. (Note: the reviewer's word 'overlapping' is overstated — the text wraps below rather than physically overlapping the wordmark — but the crowding and two-line wrap are real and present on every mobile page.)
location: packages/ui/src/site-header.tsx:23 — <nav className="flex gap-5 text-sm font-medium text-ink-muted"> has no responsive breakpoint; fed full-phrase labels from apps/parkmath/app/layout.tsx:20-25 (NAV) and apps/roammath/app/layout.tsx:20-24 (NAV)
fix: Collapse the inline nav under sm. Durable fix: wrap the desktop <nav> in `hidden sm:flex` and add an `sm:hidden` disclosure (details/summary or a button toggling a dropdown). If keeping inline links, at minimum add `whitespace-nowrap` to each <a>, drop to `gap-3`, and apply `min-w-0` to the nav — but the long first label will still strain at 390px, so the responsive collapse (or shortened single-word labels: 'Drop-off'/'Roaming') is preferred. This single fix also resolves the ragged-header-height symptom.
surface: GLOBAL CHROME (header, logo, footer, sticky mini-answer-bar, ambient backdrop) — shared across ParkMath + RoamMath

title: Header nav links fail the 44px minimum touch target
evidence: VERIFIED in code and consistent with pixels: the nav <a> elements are bare `text-sm` (14px) links with zero vertical padding (site-header.tsx:25), giving a tap height of roughly the 14px glyph + line-height (~18-20px) — well under the 44px minimum. On a 390px phone the links sit close together, so mis-taps onto an adjacent link are likely.
location: packages/ui/src/site-header.tsx:25 — <a ... className="transition-colors hover:text-brand-accent"> has no vertical padding/min-height
fix: Give each nav link a real hit area: `-my-2 py-2 inline-flex items-center min-h-11`. If moving to a mobile dropdown (Finding 1), make each menu row `min-h-12 px-4 flex items-center`.
surface: GLOBAL CHROME (header, logo, footer, sticky mini-answer-bar, ambient backdrop) — shared across ParkMath + RoamMath

title: Sticky mini-answer-bar ignores the iOS safe-area inset (home-indicator overlap)
evidence: VERIFIED in code: mini-answer-bar.tsx:30 pins `fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0` and the inner row (line 32) uses only `px-4 py-2.5` with no `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` padding. On notched iPhones the bar's content (summary + '✓ verified') can sit under the home indicator. Not directly visible in the simulator crops (which show the bar mid-page), but structurally certain from the source.
location: packages/ui/src/mini-answer-bar.tsx:30-35 — fixed bottom bar, no safe-area padding on the inner row
fix: Add bottom safe-area padding to the inner row: `pb-[max(0.625rem,env(safe-area-inset-bottom))]`. Note the bar content is informational (two <span>s, no interactive control), so this is a clipping/legibility fix rather than a tap-target fix.
surface: GLOBAL CHROME (header, logo, footer, sticky mini-answer-bar, ambient backdrop) — shared across ParkMath + RoamMath

title: Footer link row uses bare links with no tap padding
evidence: VERIFIED in code: site-footer.tsx:5-8 renders the footer nav as bare `text-sm` links inside `flex gap-4` with no padding — the same sub-44px tap-target problem as the header. The footer is below the fold in the mobile crops so it is not directly visible there; it is visible as a single tidy row in pm-home__d-full.png / rm-home__d-full.png (desktop). The reviewer's 'risk of wrapping on mobile' is speculative and not confirmed by any screenshot, so only the tap-target aspect is kept.
location: packages/ui/src/site-footer.tsx:5-8 — <nav className="flex gap-4"> with unpadded <a>
fix: Add `py-1.5 inline-flex min-h-11 items-center` to each footer link. Optionally `flex-wrap gap-x-4 gap-y-1` on the nav so it degrades gracefully if labels ever get long.
surface: GLOBAL CHROME (header, logo, footer, sticky mini-answer-bar, ambient backdrop) — shared across ParkMath + RoamMath

title: Ambient backdrop blobs use permanent 80px blur + will-change with infinite drift
evidence: VERIFIED in code: ambient-backdrop.tsx renders two large circles (420px and 360px); tokens.css:179-188 gives both `will-change: transform`, `filter: blur(80px)` and infinite 75s/90s `mf-drift` animations. Two perpetually-animating 80px-blur layers promote large compositor surfaces and can add scroll cost on budget Android. The effect is barely perceptible in the crops, so cost/benefit is weak. Confirmed: a `prefers-reduced-motion` guard already disables the blob animation (tokens.css:259-260), so the reviewer's note on that is accurate.
location: packages/ui/src/ambient-backdrop.tsx:7-14 and packages/ui/src/tokens.css:179-188 (.mf-blob / .mf-blob-2)
fix: Optional polish: also disable the drift on narrow viewports (`@media (max-width:640px){ .mf-blob,.mf-blob-2{ animation:none } }`), drop blur toward ~48-60px, and remove or scope `will-change` so the layers aren't permanently promoted. Low priority — purely a perf-hygiene nicety.
surface: GLOBAL CHROME (header, logo, footer, sticky mini-answer-bar, ambient backdrop) — shared across ParkMath + RoamMath

title: Mobile h1 (text-4xl bold) wraps to 3 lines and dominates the fold
evidence: In pm-home__m-fold.png the heading 'What does it cost to drop someone off at a UK airport?' renders at 36px (text-4xl) bold and breaks across three lines ('What does it cost to' / 'drop someone off at a' / 'UK airport?'), consuming the top third of the fold. Confirmed in source: the h1 is text-4xl on mobile with no lg step, so 36px is the mobile size. Desktop (pm-home__d-full.png) reads well at sm:text-5xl, so the issue is mobile-specific.
location: apps/parkmath/app/page.tsx:41 — h1 className="max-w-3xl text-4xl font-bold leading-tight tracking-tight text-ink sm:text-5xl"
fix: Reduce only the mobile step while preserving the existing desktop size: change text-4xl to text-3xl, keeping sm:text-5xl intact -> `text-3xl font-bold leading-tight tracking-tight text-ink sm:text-5xl`. 30px reins in the mobile dominance without altering the tablet/desktop ramp that already reads well.

title: Sticky header nav wraps — 'Drop-off charges' breaks to two lines on mobile
evidence: In pm-home__m-fold.png and m-full.png the sticky header shows the 'ParkMath' logo on the left and all four nav links inline on the right; 'Drop-off charges' visibly wraps to two lines, making the header taller and crowding the logo. Confirmed in source: site-header.tsx:23 renders `flex gap-5` with no whitespace-nowrap and no mobile collapse, and layout.tsx:20-25 passes 4 links rendered at every width. This is a sticky header at the very top of the page, so the breakage is prominent.
location: packages/ui/src/site-header.tsx:23 (nav className="flex gap-5 text-sm font-medium text-ink-muted") fed by apps/parkmath/app/layout.tsx:20-25 (NAV, 4 items)
fix: Minimum safe fix: add `whitespace-nowrap` to each link and tighten mobile gap -> `flex gap-3 sm:gap-5` on the nav. This stops the wrap at 390px. Since site-header.tsx is a shared component, verify the other apps still fit; a fuller fix (hide inline nav below sm + add a disclosure/menu button) is optional but the wrap is the priority.

title: FeeStat numbers (text-5xl) feel oversized in full-width stacked mobile cards
evidence: In pm-home__m-full.png the three navy stat cards stack full-width (the grid is only 3-up at sm) and each 48px bold figure ('£60', '23', '2') looks large for its card, with the single-digit '2' floating in a wide navy block. Confirmed: fee-stat.tsx:9 uses text-5xl unconditionally; page.tsx:52 grid is sm:grid-cols-3. Not broken or clipped — legible and intentional as hero stats — so this is a proportion/taste refinement rather than mobile breakage.
location:
packages/ui/src/fee-stat.tsx:9 — `

`
fix: Scale the value responsively: `text-4xl font-bold sm:text-5xl` so full-width mobile cards get ~36px while the desktop 3-up row keeps 48px. Verify this shared component looks right in the other apps that use FeeStat.

title: Inline 'Compare…' / 'Lounge…' links are below the 44px touch-target minimum
evidence: In pm-home__m-full.png the three cross-links ('Compare all airports in one table →', 'Compare airport parking →', 'Lounge or membership? →') render as plain underlined text with no padding; tappable height is just the ~22-24px line box, under the 44px minimum. Confirmed in source: page.tsx:59, 65, 68 are text-base Links with no vertical padding, and the latter two sit inside `flex flex-wrap gap-6` so they can crowd on one row.
location:
apps/parkmath/app/page.tsx:58-71 — three Link elements (one standalone, two inside `

`)
fix: Give each link a real tap area: add `inline-flex min-h-11 items-center` (min-h-11 = 44px) to each Link, and add vertical breathing room to the wrapping row with `gap-x-6 gap-y-2`.

title: Subhead lead paragraph at text-lg (18px) is heavy stacked under the oversized h1 on mobile
evidence: In pm-home__m-fold.png the description paragraph wraps to ~4 lines at 18px directly under the already-dominant h1, adding to the top-heavy fold. Confirmed: page.tsx:44 uses text-lg with no mobile step. 18px is fine on desktop; on a 390px phone under a big heading it compounds the heaviness. Minor rhythm refinement, not breakage.
location:
apps/parkmath/app/page.tsx:44 — `

`
fix: Step the lead down on mobile: `text-base sm:text-lg`. 16px on mobile tightens the fold while keeping the 18px lead on larger screens.

title: Search field has no leading magnifier icon — affordance relies on placeholder alone
evidence: In all three screenshots the search input is a plain rounded box with only placeholder text ('Search your airport — e.g. Gatwick or LGW') and no leading search glyph. Confirmed in source: airport-search.tsx:16-23 is a bare input with px-4 py-3.5 and no icon wrapper. A leading magnifier is the conventional affordance for a primary search and aids scannability, especially on mobile. Pure enhancement.
location: apps/parkmath/components/airport-search.tsx:16-23 — input with `px-4 py-3.5` and no icon
fix: Wrap the input in a `relative` container, add an absolutely-positioned search SVG (`absolute left-3.5 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 text-ink-muted`), and bump input left padding to `pl-11`, preserving the existing focus ring/border.

title: Long airport names wrap to two lines in the 2-col mobile result grid
evidence: In pm-home__m-full.png the result grid is 2-col on mobile and longer names ('Belfast International', 'Liverpool John Lennon', 'Teesside International') wrap to two lines in the narrow p-3.5 cards. Confirmed in source: airport-search.tsx:24 grid-cols-2, :32 text-sm font-semibold. However the cards use `h-full flex flex-col justify-between` and grid rows auto-equalize height, so rows stay aligned and the fee line does not collide with names — purely a cosmetic tidiness nit.
location: apps/parkmath/components/airport-search.tsx:24 (grid-cols-2) and :32 (text-sm font-semibold), cards p-3.5 at line 29
fix: Optional polish only — keep grid-cols-2 (it reads fine). If desired, even the wraps with `text-balance` or a slightly smaller name (`text-[0.8125rem]`) on mobile. Low priority; no functional problem.

title: Drop-off table overflows at 390px with no scroll affordance — header clips mid-word ('PENALT…'), Penalty/Free-alt/Verified columns off-screen
evidence: In pm-dropoff-table__m-full.png the header row reads AIRPORT, FEE, TIME LIMIT, then 'PENALT…' clipped mid-word at the right edge; the £ penalty values (£10, £8, £9, £5) are half-cut, and Free alternative + Verified are entirely off-screen. The parent (fee-grid.tsx:16 'overflow-hidden rounded-card') clips the scrollbar of the inner overflow-x-auto (:19), so there is no fade/scrollbar/hint that more columns exist. Columns are technically swipe-reachable, but for the 6-column 'compare everything' value prop on the dominant mobile viewport there is zero discoverable cue and the clipped-mid-word header reads as broken. Desktop (pm-dropoff-table__d-full.png) fits all 6 columns fine, so this is mobile-only.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:16 (wrapper 'mf-edge overflow-hidden rounded-card') + :19 ('overflow-x-auto') on a 'w-full' table with no responsive collapse; 6 columns from apps/parkmath/app/drop-off-charges/page.tsx:50
fix: Card-ify rows below sm: render each row as a stacked card (airport name as title, each remaining column as a label/value pair) behind 'sm:hidden', keep the <table> at 'hidden sm:block'. This removes horizontal scroll on mobile entirely and is strongly preferred for a 6-column data table at 390px. If the table form is retained instead, move the rounded corners/border off the clipping wrapper (or drop 'overflow-hidden' on :16) so the scroll region is not clipped, give the table a min-width to force a real scroll region, freeze the first column ('sticky left-0 bg-white z-10'), and add a right-edge gradient fade + 'Swipe to compare →' hint ('text-xs text-ink-muted sm:hidden').

title: Parking table 'Cheapest 7-day option' cell wraps to 4–5 lines, producing very tall ragged rows on mobile
evidence: In pm-parking-table__m-full.png the second column holds full descriptive strings ('Park & Ride (formerly Long Stay) — drive-up', 'Turn Up & Park — T2 West Multi Storey (P3)', 'Long Stay — on-the-day (drive-up)') that wrap into 4–5 stacked lines in a narrow column, making each row roughly 120–150px tall. The table reads as a stack of tall ragged blocks rather than a scannable comparison, and because fee-grid.tsx:41 forces every non-first cell to text-right + mf-num, this prose is also right-aligned which worsens the ragged look. Content is not clipped or lost, so this is degraded readability rather than a hard breakage.
location: apps/parkmath/app/airport-parking/page.tsx:49,54 (column 'Cheapest 7-day option' + r.cheapest?.name long strings) rendered by packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:41
fix: Card-ify on mobile so the option name gets full row width under a 'Cheapest 7-day option' label, eliminating the narrow-column wrap. If keeping a table, clamp the string (line-clamp-2) and/or demote the parenthetical detail ('formerly Long Stay', 'drive-up') to a smaller secondary line, and left-align this non-numeric column (see the alignment fix below).

title: Non-numeric columns ('Free alternative', 'Cheapest 7-day option') are force-styled as right-aligned numeric cells
evidence: fee-grid.tsx:41 applies 'mf-num px-5 py-3.5 text-right text-ink-muted' to EVERY non-first cell, with no per-column override. In pm-dropoff-table__d-full.png the 'Free alternative' column shows prose like 'Long Stay car park (120 min)' / 'Mid Stay car park drop zone (60 min)' right-aligned and tabular-numbered, which is the wrong treatment for text — the (xx min) parenthetical competes with the name and it reads awkwardly. The same forced text-right hits the parking 'Cheapest 7-day option' prose.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:41 (all non-first cells forced text-right + mf-num) + apps/parkmath/app/drop-off-charges/page.tsx:60 (freeAlternative string)
fix: Add a per-column type/alignment to FeeGrid (e.g. a parallel 'align' or 'numeric' array) so text columns like 'Free alternative' and 'Cheapest 7-day option' render left-aligned, normal weight, without mf-num, while price/penalty columns keep text-right mf-num. On mobile these belong as their own labeled line in the card layout.

title: Page h1 is text-3xl bold and wraps to two lines on mobile, reading heavy for the calm 'instrument' tone
evidence: All three index pages use 'text-3xl font-bold text-ink' for the h1 (drop-off-charges/page.tsx:45, airport-parking/page.tsx:44, airport-lounges/page.tsx:29). In the mobile screenshots each headline wraps to two lines and dominates the first viewport before any data. This is a tone/polish judgement, not a layout breakage — text is not clipped and nothing overflows.
location: apps/parkmath/app/drop-off-charges/page.tsx:45; apps/parkmath/app/airport-parking/page.tsx:44; apps/parkmath/app/airport-lounges/page.tsx:29
fix: Make the heading responsive and lighter on small screens: 'text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight sm:text-3xl sm:font-bold', optionally add 'text-balance'. Extract a shared PageHeading so all three pages stay in lockstep.

title: Fixed px-5 py-3.5 cell padding is not responsive — consumes horizontal budget that contributes to the mobile overflow
evidence: fee-grid.tsx:25,39,41 hard-code px-5 (20px each side = 40px per column). With 6 columns in the drop-off table that is ~240px of horizontal padding on a 390px screen, a real contributor to the overflow in finding 1. This is a contributing factor, not a standalone breakage.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:25,39,41 (px-5 py-3.5 hard-coded on th/td)
fix: Scale padding by viewport: 'px-3 py-3 sm:px-5 sm:py-3.5'. This is a stopgap that buys horizontal room; the real fix for the 6-column table remains the card-ify approach in finding 1.

title: Mobile airport names wrap to two lines while values stay single-line, so fee values float mis-aligned
evidence: In pm-dropoff-table__m-full.png first-column links wrap ('London Gatwick', 'London Stansted', 'Liverpool John Lennon', 'Teesside International' all break to two lines) while the numeric £10/£8 in the same row sit on one line. Because cells are not top-aligned, the fee value centers against a two-line name and looks ragged. Minor polish, not breakage.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:39 (first-column th, no vertical-align) + :41 (value cells); long names from data
fix: In the card-ify layout the name becomes a full-width title and this disappears. If the table is retained, top-align cells ('align-top' on th/td) so the value lines up with the first line of the name. Do not force nowrap (would worsen overflow).

title: Site nav label 'Drop-off charges' wraps under the wordmark on mobile
evidence: Across all three mobile screenshots the top nav shows the ParkMath logo with 'Drop-off charges' broken onto a second line ('Drop-off' / 'charges') while 'Parking Lounges Privacy' sit on the first row, crowding the header. Visible and real, but it lives in the shared site header/layout, not in any of this surface's four files, so it is more properly owned by a header/nav surface review.
location: Shared site header/nav for apps/parkmath (not in fee-grid.tsx or the three index pages). Visible in pm-dropoff-table__m-full.png, pm-parking-table__m-full.png, pm-lounge-table__m-full.png.
fix: In the header component, add 'whitespace-nowrap' to each nav link and adopt a mobile-appropriate nav (collapse the four links into a menu, shorten 'Drop-off charges' to 'Drop-off' below sm, or allow the nav row to scroll horizontally). Confirm in the parkmath header/layout file.

title: Site header has no mobile nav — 4 links wrap and collide with the wordmark
evidence: Verified in every mobile shot (pm-dropoff-heathrow__m-full/__m-fold, pm-dropoff-gatwick__m-full, pm-parking-heathrow__m-full, pm-parking-7day__m-full, pm-lounge-heathrow__m-full): the 'ParkMath' wordmark and the four nav links (Drop-off charges / Parking / Lounges / Privacy) share one row; 'Drop-off charges' breaks mid-word to a second line ('Drop-' / 'off charges') directly beneath/colliding with the logo. Reads as broken on the single most-seen element of a 96%-mobile audience.
location: packages/ui/src/site-header.tsx lines 17-27 — one `flex ... justify-between` row holds `<a>` wordmark and `<nav className="flex gap-5 text-sm ...">` with all links always visible; no breakpoint, no menu, logo not `shrink-0`, row not `flex-nowrap`.
fix: Hide the inline nav on mobile and add a disclosure menu: `<nav className="hidden gap-5 sm:flex ...">` plus a hamburger `<button className="sm:hidden ...">` (>=44px tap target) toggling a dropdown/sheet. Keep the wordmark `shrink-0`. Ensure the row never wraps (`flex-nowrap min-w-0`).
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: Caveat chip contains a whole sentence and wraps to multiple lines
evidence: Verified on pm-dropoff-heathrow (__m-full, __m-fold, __t-fold) and pm-dropoff-gatwick__m-full: the amber rounded-full pill reads 'Pay by Midnight the day after your visit (can also pay in advance or on the day)' — a full sentence that wraps to two lines on mobile and runs nearly card-edge-to-edge on tablet. A wrapping pill reads as a broken tag, breaking the terse-chip aesthetic the page leans on (the Max-stay / penalty chips next to it are terse).
location: apps/parkmath/app/drop-off-charges/[airport]/page.tsx line 67 `<CaveatChip>Pay by {record.paymentDeadline}</CaveatChip>` (paymentDeadline is a full sentence in the dataset); chip has no max-width/truncation in packages/ui/src/caveat-chip.tsx (rounded-full, px-2.5 py-1).
fix: Keep the chip terse — render a short token (e.g. 'Pay next day' or 'Pay by midnight') and move the parenthetical detail into the AnswerLead bullet list (where 'Pay by: ...' already appears, page.tsx line 81) or a tooltip. Structurally, shorten `record.paymentDeadline` in the dataset or pass a separate `detail` prop rendered outside the pill.
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: Parking hub FeeGrid (4-column) overflows on mobile — 14-day price column clipped at card edge
evidence: Verified on pm-parking-heathrow__m-full: the 4-column FeeGrid (Option / 3 days / 7 days / 14 days) with first-column label 'Park & Ride (formerly Long Stay) — drive-up' forces the table wider than the card; the right-hand 14-day price is cut off at the card edge. `overflow-x-auto` turns this into a silent horizontal scroll on a financial page where the figure is the point. NOTE: the 3-column duration FeeGrid on pm-parking-7day__m-full does NOT clip (£271.20 fully visible) — the original report's 7day citation is incorrect; only the 4-column hub grid overflows.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx lines 19-20 (`overflow-x-auto` + `w-full` table, no responsive stacked layout); fed by apps/parkmath/app/airport-parking/[airport]/page.tsx lines 86-104 with long `p.name` row labels.
fix: Below `sm`, render a stacked card list (label on top; the duration prices as a key/value row) and only show the `<table>` at `sm:` and up. If keeping the table, shorten the row label (move '(formerly Long Stay) — drive-up' to a muted sub-label) and add a visible scroll affordance (edge fade).
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: Long parking option name wraps heavily in the calculator winner row
evidence: Verified on pm-parking-heathrow__m-full: the calculator 'Cheapest' winner row label 'Park & Ride (formerly Long Stay) — drive-up' wraps to ~3 lines next to the £271.20 price; the `items-baseline` row keeps the price pinned high while the name stack grows, breaking the clean name|price alignment. The product name is real dataset text (packages/data/datasets/parkmath/parking-tariffs.json line 10), so it recurs on every Heathrow render. Downgraded to medium: it is messy wrap, not clipping or breakage.
location: apps/parkmath/components/parking-calculator.tsx lines 49-55 (`flex items-baseline justify-between`, full `o.name`, price `text-lg ... text-brand` with no `shrink-0` / `min-w-0` guards).
fix: Split a bold primary name from a muted sub-label ('Park & Ride' bold; '(formerly Long Stay) · drive-up' `text-xs text-ink-muted` on a second line), switch the row to `items-start`, give the name `min-w-0` and the price `shrink-0`. Best long-term: shorten the canonical product label in the dataset.
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: AnswerLead and AnswerCard repeat the same sentence verbatim — muddled hierarchy
evidence: Verified on pm-dropoff-heathrow__m-full and __d-full: the bordered AnswerLead 'Dropping off at London Heathrow costs £7 each time a vehicle enters a terminal drop-off zone' is immediately followed by the navy AnswerCard whose note repeats '£7 each time a vehicle enters a terminal drop-off zone'. Both consume `record.feeSummary` (page.tsx line 75 vs line 89), so the same sentence and the £7 fact appear twice within one viewport, and the navy hero competes with the bordered lead for 'the answer'.
location: apps/parkmath/app/drop-off-charges/[airport]/page.tsx lines 71-95 (AnswerLead `answer` derived from `record.feeSummary`; AnswerCard `note={record.feeSummary}`).
fix: Pick one hero. Either drop the AnswerCard `note` (the feeSummary is already shown in the lead) so the card carries only the figure + stamp, or shorten the AnswerLead to context only and let the navy card own the number. Each block should carry distinct information (lead = context, card = the figure).
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: h1 wraps with a mid-word 'drop-off' break on mobile
evidence: Verified on pm-dropoff-heathrow__m-full ('London Heathrow drop-' / 'off charge' — broken mid-word at the hyphen), pm-parking-7day__m-full ('7-day parking at London' / 'Heathrow') and pm-lounge-heathrow__m-full ('London Heathrow lounges: pay per visit or join?' over 2-3 lines). The `text-3xl` (30px) bold h1 is the heaviest element at 390px and the mid-word 'drop-/off' split looks broken. (Correction: drop-off and parking wrap to 2 lines, not 3 as originally reported.)
location: apps/parkmath/app/drop-off-charges/[airport]/page.tsx line 56 and app/airport-parking/[airport]/page.tsx line 59 (`text-3xl font-bold`); lounge page h1 same pattern.
fix: Responsive clamp `text-2xl sm:text-3xl` (24px mobile -> 30px up), add `text-balance` to even the wrap, and prevent the hyphen split with `[overflow-wrap:normal]` / a non-breaking treatment on 'drop-off' (e.g. wrap it in a `whitespace-nowrap` span).
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: Duration toggle buttons are under the 44px touch-target minimum
evidence: Verified in code and visible on pm-parking-heathrow__m-full: the day-selector pills (1 day / 3 days / 7 days / 14 days / 28 days) render at `px-4 py-2 text-sm` (~36px tall), below the 44px iOS/WCAG comfortable target. These are the primary interaction on a mobile-first calculator. (Note: at 390px all five fit on one row, so the separate 'toggle wraps' concern does not reproduce — only the target height is the real issue.)
location: apps/parkmath/components/parking-calculator.tsx lines 28-29 (`px-4 py-2`).
fix: Bump to `min-h-11 px-4 py-2.5` (44px) on mobile (or `py-3 sm:py-2`). Keep the five segments on one row.
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: Lounge slider lacks the focus-visible ring and active glow the drop-off slider has
evidence: Verified in code: drop-off-calculator.tsx line 39 gives the range input `focus-visible:shadow-[...accent ring...]` plus an `active:shadow-[...]` glow; lounge-calculator.tsx line 19 uses only `accent-brand-accent` with no focus or active state. Two sibling calculators on the same family of pages behave differently on keyboard focus — a polish/consistency gap (visible on pm-lounge-heathrow__m-full only as the absence of a focus affordance, hence low).
location: apps/parkmath/components/lounge-calculator.tsx lines 18-19 vs apps/parkmath/components/drop-off-calculator.tsx line 39.
fix: Extract the slider className into a shared constant (or a small `<RangeSlider>` UI component) and apply it to both calculators so focus ring, active glow and hit area are identical.
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: Parking warnings render as 12px muted fine-print while drop-off uses amber chips — inconsistent caveat treatment
evidence: Verified in code: the drop-off calculator surfaces warnings as amber CaveatChips (drop-off-calculator.tsx line 50), but the parking page and parking calculator render the same class of caveat as bare `text-xs text-ink-muted` list items with no container (parking-calculator.tsx lines 60-64; airport-parking/[airport]/page.tsx lines 78-84). On the brand's 'we surface what others bury' premise, the parking caveats read as stray fine print and feel less finished than the drop-off page. (Severity low: warnings are often empty for the default 7-day view, so impact is intermittent.)
location: apps/parkmath/components/parking-calculator.tsx lines 60-64 and apps/parkmath/app/airport-parking/[airport]/page.tsx lines 78-84 (`text-xs text-ink-muted`).
fix: Render parking warnings with the same CaveatChip treatment as the drop-off calculator, or at least lift them to `text-sm` in a bordered note. Avoid 12px muted body for meaningful caveats.
surface: ParkMath detail pages (drop-off charge, parking comparison, 7-day duration, lounge break-even) — mobile 390px primary, plus desktop/tablet

title: 6-column fee table overflows horizontally at 390px — half the columns clipped, no scroll affordance
evidence: Verified in rm-table-crop1.png and rm-roaming-table__m-full.png: at 390px the table shows only DESTINATION, EE and O2; O2 is already touching the right edge and Vodafone, Three and 'Best eSIM (5GB/7d)' are entirely off-screen. The comparison columns — the whole point of the page — are reachable only by horizontal scroll, and there is no visible scroll affordance (no fade/shadow on the right edge). Confirmed in source: FeeGrid wraps a `table w-full` in `overflow-x-auto` with 6 columns and px-5 py-3.5 cells.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:19-47 (overflow-x-auto wrapper + w-full table, px-5 py-3.5 cells); columns fed from apps/roammath/app/roaming/page.tsx:113-117 (6 columns)
fix: Adopt a responsive pattern rather than a 6-col scroll grid. Best: render the <table> only at md+ (`hidden md:block`) and add a mobile stacked card list (flag + name, then EE/O2/Vodafone/Three/eSIM as label:value rows). Cheaper interim: reduce per-cell padding on mobile (`px-2 sm:px-5`), and add a right-edge gradient affordance on the overflow-x-auto wrapper (`after:pointer-events-none after:absolute after:inset-y-0 after:right-0 after:w-8 after:bg-gradient-to-l after:from-white`) so users know to scroll. This wrapper is shared (ParkMath has the same defect), so fix once in fee-grid.tsx.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Table caption is clipped mid-word by the horizontal-scroll container
evidence: Verified in rm-table-crop1.png: the caption reads 'Daily charge per network. ʼincludedʼ = no extra cost. eSIM colur…' — cut off mid-word ('colour') at the right edge. Confirmed in source: the <caption> lives inside the same overflow-x-auto table (fee-grid.tsx:21) that is wider than the viewport, so it inherits the table's overflow width instead of wrapping. Downgraded from high to medium: this is loss of a secondary explanatory helper line, not breakage of the primary data, so it is a legibility nuisance rather than a critical/high failure.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:21 (caption inside the scrolling table); caption text from apps/roammath/app/roaming/page.tsx:114
fix:
Move the caption out of the scrolling table into a normal-flow

above the overflow-x-auto wrapper so it wraps to viewport width, e.g. `

` rendered as a sibling before the `

`. Keep a visually-hidden inside the table for a11y.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Hero heading text-4xl is oversized on mobile — wraps and dominates the fold
evidence: Verified in rm-home-crop1.png / rm-home__m-fold.png: the h1 'What does your phone cost abroad?' at text-4xl (36px) bold wraps to multiple lines and takes a large share of the first screen, matching the user's 'fonts look too big' report. (Note: it wraps to ~2 lines, not 3 as the original report stated.) Downgraded from high to medium — this is typographic taste/calibration on a single page, not a layout breakage.
location: apps/roammath/app/page.tsx:72 (`text-4xl font-bold text-ink`)
fix: Make the hero responsive and tighten the large weight: `text-3xl sm:text-4xl tracking-tight` (or `text-[28px] leading-tight sm:text-4xl`). Preserves desktop impact while calming the mobile fold.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Stat-card numbers at text-5xl are oversized for 390px; the '£0 (O2)' compound reads awkwardly
evidence: Verified in rm-home__m-fold.png: the three FeeStat values render at text-5xl (48px) leading-none: '40', '4 + 3' and '£0 (O2)'. On a single-column mobile stack these feel shouty, and the third value is a number+network-label compound — at 48px bold the '(O2)' (which is data, not a headline number) looks unbalanced. Confirmed in source: fee-stat.tsx renders the value as `text-5xl font-bold leading-none`. Borderline low/medium; kept at medium.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-stat.tsx:9 (`mf-num mt-2 text-5xl font-bold leading-none`); value strings from apps/roammath/app/page.tsx:84-98
fix: Scale the value responsively (`text-4xl sm:text-5xl`). For the Spain card, split the value into a hero number plus a smaller muted qualifier (e.g. '£0' at text-5xl and '(O2)' in a `text-xl font-semibold text-white/70` span) rather than one 48px string.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Header nav 'Roaming charges' link wraps to two lines on mobile, looking broken next to single-line siblings
evidence: Verified in rm-home__m-fold.png: in the sticky header the RoamMath logo is followed by 'Roaming charges' breaking onto two stacked lines, then 'Baggage fees' and 'Privacy', all squeezed across the 390px bar. The wrapped two-line link looks broken beside single-line siblings. Confirmed in source: the nav uses `flex gap-5 text-sm font-medium` with NO whitespace-nowrap, and packs BrandLogo + three multi-word links at 390px. This is global chrome (every page), which argues toward high, but it is a header label-wrap rather than content loss, so medium.
location: packages/ui/src/site-header.tsx:23-27 (`<nav ... className="flex gap-5 text-sm">`; links from apps/roammath/app/layout.tsx:20-24)
fix: Add `whitespace-nowrap` to the nav links and tighten the mobile gap (`gap-3 sm:gap-5`), and/or shorten the mobile label to 'Roaming'. If logo + three links still cannot fit one row at 390px, collapse to a compact menu pattern. Fix in the shared site-header.tsx (affects all RoamMath pages, and ParkMath if it reuses the component).
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Home 'destinations' list is plain underlined text links — inconsistent with the premium flag-chip grid on the roaming index
evidence: Verified: the roaming index (rm-roaming-table__m-fold.png) presents destinations as polished rounded flag-chips (border, white bg, flag + name). The home page (rm-home-header.png crop + rm-home__m-full.png) renders the same 40 destinations as a bare two-column list of teal underlined text links with no flags. Confirmed in source: home uses `<ul className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 text-sm sm:grid-cols-4">` of underlined Links (page.tsx:103-114) vs the flag-chip nav in roaming/page.tsx:100-111. Two visual languages for identical content.
location: apps/roammath/app/page.tsx:103-114 (underlined Link list) vs apps/roammath/app/roaming/page.tsx:100-111 (flag-chip nav)
fix: Unify on the flag-chip pattern: reuse CountryFlag + the rounded-full bordered chip on the home destinations section, OR drop the full 40-item list on home in favour of a few featured chips plus the existing 'Compare all roaming charges →' link. Extract the chip into a shared component so home and index stay in sync.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Home destination text-links are under the 44px touch target and visually cramped
evidence: Verified in rm-home-header.png: the home destination links ('Spain', 'France', 'Italy', 'Portugal'…) are text-sm links in a grid with `gap-2` and no block padding, so each tap target is roughly the line-box (~20px), well under the 44px minimum, and adjacent rows sit close enough to risk mis-taps at 390px. Confirmed in source: page.tsx:103-111 — links have no block/padding class. (Grid is grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4.)
location: apps/roammath/app/page.tsx:103-111 (`grid grid-cols-2 gap-2 text-sm sm:grid-cols-4`, links have no block padding)
fix: Give each link a block-level tap area meeting 44px: `block py-2.5` (or convert to the flag-chip with `py-1.5 px-3` like the index). If kept as a list, `inline-flex min-h-[44px] items-center`. Note: fixing finding 6 (reuse the chip) resolves this simultaneously.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Flag-chip tap height is under the 44px touch-target floor at 390px
evidence: Verified in rm-roaming-table__m-fold.png: the flag-chips are a flex-wrap row, mostly 2–3 per line; density and look are good. Confirmed in source: chips use `px-3 py-1.5 text-sm` (~32px tall) which is under the 44px touch floor. The 'ragged wrap' on long labels (e.g. 'United Arab Emirates' taking a full-width chip) is cosmetic and acceptable for a flex-wrap nav.
location: apps/roammath/app/roaming/page.tsx:100-111 (`flex flex-wrap gap-2`, chips `px-3 py-1.5 text-sm`)
fix: Bump vertical padding to `py-2` or add `min-h-[40px]` (ideally `min-h-[44px]`) so chips clear the touch-target floor with minimal visual change. Leave the ragged wrap as-is.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Table numeric cells are muted (text-ink-muted) — the key comparison data is the lowest-contrast text on the row
evidence: Verified in rm-table-crop1.png/rm-table-crop2.png: price/'included' values render in muted grey, lighter than the bold teal destination names, so the actual comparison data (the reason for the table) has the weakest contrast on the row, inverting the intended hierarchy. On rm-roaming-table__d-full.png the same muted grey makes the dense number grid feel faint. Confirmed in source: fee-grid.tsx:41 applies `text-ink-muted` to every numeric <td>.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:41 (`text-ink-muted` on every numeric <td>)
fix: Give the data its own weight: use `text-ink` (optionally `font-medium`) for numeric cells and reserve muted grey for secondary qualifiers like 'included'. Optionally highlight the cheapest cell per row in brand-accent for scannable hierarchy. Shared component — affects ParkMath tables too.
surface: RoamMath home + roaming destination index (flag-chip grid + per-network fee table)

title: Country/airline h1 is text-3xl with no responsive step-down — wraps to 3 lines on mobile
evidence: rm-spain__m-fold.png: the h1 'Spain roaming charges: all four UK networks compared' wraps to 3 dense 30px lines and fills most of the fold. rm-baggage-ryanair__m-full.png: 'Ryanair baggage fees: official published charges' wraps to 3 lines. (Note: the network h1 'EE roaming in Spain' in rm-network__m-full.png is short and wraps only to ~2 lines, so the breakage is on the long country/baggage titles, not the network one.) globals.css has no h1 rule and no text-balance utility, so text-3xl is fixed at every breakpoint.
location: apps/roammath/app/roaming/[country]/page.tsx:102; baggage-fees/page.tsx:39; baggage-fees/[airline]/page.tsx:74 — h1 className="text-3xl font-bold text-ink". Verified globals.css defines no h1/text-balance.
fix: Make the heading fluid and balance the wrap: className="text-2xl font-bold tracking-tight text-balance text-ink sm:text-3xl". 24px base on mobile removes the cramped 3-line block while keeping 30px at >=640px. Apply to all four h1s for consistency.

title: FeeGrid blanket-right-aligns every non-first column, misaligning prose Notes / Pass-name / Fair-use columns
evidence: fee-grid.tsx forces text-right on the th (j>0) and on every td (j>0). rm-baggage-ryanair__d-full.png / __m-full.png: the 'NOTES' column ('Online price; varies by route and date', 'Included with every fare') is right-aligned prose, reading ragged-left. rm-spain__d-full.png: the 'PASS / PRODUCT NAME' and 'FAIR-USE NOTE' text columns are also right-aligned. Right-alignment is correct for the numeric Price/Daily-charge columns, wrong for the text columns.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:25 (th) and :41 (td) — hardcode `text-right` for every j>0 column
fix: Add an optional prop (e.g. numericColumns?: number[] or per-column align) so only price/charge columns get text-right + mf-num; keep name/note/fair-use columns text-left. Default behaviour can stay numeric-right but the prose columns on these tables should opt out.

title: Networks roaming table crams 4 columns incl. long Fair-use prose into 390px — cramped, scroll-hides a column
evidence: rm-spain__m-full.png: the 4-column table (Network / Daily charge / Pass-product name / Fair-use note) is squeezed into the mobile column with px-5 (20px) cell padding; the Fair-use column holds full sentences ('50GB Europe roaming fair-use cap...') that wrap to many lines, making rows tall and the grid claustrophobic. The overflow-x-auto wrapper also lets the Fair-use column scroll off-screen on a trust-led financial table.
location: packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:19 (overflow-x-auto) + :25/:39/:41 (px-5 py-3.5 cells); consumed by apps/roammath/app/roaming/[country]/page.tsx:128-137 (4 columns incl. long Fair-use note)
fix: (a) Tighten cell padding responsively in FeeGrid: px-3 py-3 sm:px-5 sm:py-3.5. (b) For the roaming table, cap the Fair-use cell width and shrink its type (td className 'whitespace-normal max-w-[9rem] text-xs') or move the Fair-use sentence below the table as a footnote on mobile. Long prose does not belong in a numeric column at 390px.

title: AnswerLead lead paragraph is text-xl semibold — a second heavy 'headline' stacked under the h1 on mobile
evidence: rm-spain__m-fold.png: directly below the 3-line h1, AnswerLead renders a multi-line 20px semibold paragraph ('O2 customers roam in Spain at no extra daily charge (fair-use limits apply)...'), then a bulleted list. Two stacked heavy type blocks fill the fold with bold text and no calm whitespace.
location:
packages/ui/src/answer-lead.tsx:7 —

; consumed at roaming/[country]/page.tsx:113 and baggage-fees/[airline]/page.tsx:81
fix: Step the lead down and lighten weight on mobile: className="text-base font-medium leading-relaxed text-ink sm:text-lg". Reserve semibold/text-xl for >=640px so there is one clear h1 then a calmer supporting lead.

title: AnswerLead bullets carry long full sentences with fair-use clauses — dense small-text block on mobile
evidence: rm-spain__m-fold.png: each bullet is a full sentence ('EE: £2.59/day (Daily EU Charge); EU/EEA/Switzerland zone; 50GB Europe roaming fair-use cap (EE help). Included at no charge for plans launched before 7 Jul 2021.'). Five such bullets at text-sm create a dense grey wall directly under the lead, hard to scan on a 390px column.
location: apps/roammath/app/roaming/[country]/page.tsx:59-70 (networkFacts strings) rendered by answer-lead.tsx:9-15
fix: Safe CSS: add leading-relaxed to the <ul>/<li> in answer-lead.tsx so bullets breathe. Optional (content, verify with data owner): trim networkFacts to a figure-first fragment (network + price + one caveat) and move launch-date/zone detail into the table or an expandable; bold the network label so bullets read as key:value, not prose.

title: Network-specific page links wrap into a ragged underlined run-on row with small tap targets
evidence: rm-spain__m-full.png 'Network-specific pages': four underlined links 'EE roaming in Spain →', 'O2 roaming in Spain →' etc. sit in a flex-wrap gap-3 row and wrap unevenly into a ragged underlined block that reads like body text, not navigation, with no clear per-link tap separation.
location: apps/roammath/app/roaming/[country]/page.tsx:141-152 — <ul className="flex flex-wrap gap-3 text-sm"> of underlined Links
fix: Stack vertically on mobile and give each link a tappable row: ul className="grid gap-2 sm:flex sm:flex-wrap sm:gap-3" and add py-2 to each Link so it is a >=44px target. Optionally shorten the label to '{Network} →' since the country is the page context.

title: MiniAnswerBar uses a raw ✓ text glyph instead of the brand SVG tick used by FreshnessBadge
evidence: FreshnessBadge renders a crafted SVG checkmark path (the green 'Verified 10 Jun 2026' pill in rm-spain__m-fold.png). The sticky teal MiniAnswerBar (visible in rm-spain__m-full.png) renders a literal '✓ verified' text glyph in emerald-300. The font glyph is visually inconsistent with the brand SVG tick and renders differently across platforms on a trust-led product.
location: packages/ui/src/mini-answer-bar.tsx:34 — ✓ verified vs packages/ui/src/freshness-badge.tsx:23-25 (SVG tick path)
fix: Extract the FreshnessBadge SVG tick into a shared <CheckTick/> and reuse it in MiniAnswerBar at h-3 w-3 so the verified mark is pixel-identical everywhere.

title: Baggage index table is dominated by right-aligned em-dashes and wrapping 'Not published' — sparse grid
evidence: rm-baggage-table__m-full.png: the Cabin bag / First checked bag columns show many '—' cells (Jet2.com, British Airways, Aer Lingus cabin; several checked) plus 'Not published' wrapping to two lines (Wizz Air, Norwegian). Right-aligned em-dashes against airline names read as an empty, ragged grid that undercuts the 'definitive comparison' framing.
location: apps/roammath/app/baggage-fees/page.tsx:48-59 (rows) + feeRangeLabel returning '—'/'Not published'; rendered via fee-grid.tsx (right-aligned cells)
fix: De-emphasise missing values consistently (muted '—') and stop 'Not published' wrapping (whitespace-nowrap text-xs). Optionally style 'Free' as a small positive badge so the table reads as informative rather than empty. (The 4th 'Verified' column also reuses the date as a numeric-style cell — fine, leave as is.)

title: 'FIRST CHECKED BAG' column header wraps to two lines, making the baggage thead row uneven
evidence: rm-baggage-table__m-full.png: in the uppercase tracking-wider thead the header 'FIRST CHECKED BAG' wraps to two lines while 'CABIN BAG' stays one line, making the header row uneven and cramped at px-5 padding on 390px.
location: apps/roammath/app/baggage-fees/page.tsx:47 (columns array) + packages/ui/src/fee-grid.tsx:22-25 (thead uppercase tracking-wider px-5)
fix: Shorten the header to 'Checked bag' ('first' is implied) and reduce thead padding on mobile (px-3 sm:px-5); optionally tracking-normal on mobile to avoid the wrap so each header is one line.

title: Faint 260px flag watermark sits behind the mobile h1, reading as a print smudge
evidence: rm-spain__m-fold.png shows a faint reddish/grey smudge behind the right side of the heading (the 260px opacity-0.06 Spain flag, absolute -top-10 right-0). Behind the tall 3-line heading on a 390px column it reads as a faint artifact rather than premium texture. The 36px inline flag at page.tsx:101 already brands the header on mobile, so the watermark adds little there.
location: apps/roammath/app/roaming/[country]/page.tsx:95-99 — <CountryFlag size={260} className="pointer-events-none absolute -top-10 right-0 opacity-[0.06]" />
fix: Hide or shrink the watermark on mobile and pull it clear of the text: className="pointer-events-none absolute -top-12 -right-8 hidden opacity-[0.05] sm:block" (or size={180} until sm). Low priority — it is subtle.

title: Header freshness pill + dotted SourceCitation crowd into one wrapping row under a heavy h1
evidence: rm-spain__m-fold.png: the 'Verified 10 Jun 2026' solid pill and the 'EE price guide ↗' dotted-underline citation share a flex-wrap items-center gap-3 row directly beneath the 3-line h1; the baggage detail header repeats the pattern ('Official Ryanair fee page ↗'). The solid pill next to the dotted citation reads slightly busy under an already-heavy heading.
location: apps/roammath/app/roaming/[country]/page.tsx:104-109 and baggage-fees/[airline]/page.tsx:75-78 — flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3
fix: Largely resolves once the h1 is stepped down (finding 1). Additionally add gap-y-2 for clean wrap; the inline SourceCitation is duplicated in the bottom Sources block, so hiding it on mobile (hidden sm:inline-flex) keeps the sub-head to a single calm freshness pill.

title: Calculator verdict line is text-base font-bold in a tinted strip — adds another bold block on a type-heavy page
evidence: rm-spain__m-full.png: the calculator verdict ('O2 customers pay nothing extra here.') renders as bold brand-coloured text-base in a teal-tinted strip directly under lighter result rows, stacking one more bold emphasis onto an already type-heavy fold.
location: apps/roammath/components/roaming-calculator.tsx:55 — className="...p-3 text-base font-bold text-brand"
fix: Soften to text-sm font-semibold (keep the brand colour and tint) so the verdict reads as a confident conclusion rather than a banner. Pure polish; pairs with stepping down the h1/lead.
